2606.19847.title: AtomMem: Building Simple and Effective Memory System for LLM Agents via Atomic Facts
2606.19847.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate strong reasoning and generation abilities, but their fixed context windows limit long-term information accumulation and reuse across multi-session interactions. Existing memory-augmented systems often construct memory in a coarse and unstable manner, relying on inefficient memory representations or unstable unconstrained updates. To address these challenges, we propose AtomMem, a long-term memory system designed for value-dense storage and stable memory evolution. AtomMem introduces a Fact Executor, which selectively extracts high value atomic facts from long form interactions to serve as highly efficient memory representations. Subsequently, AtomMem organizes these facts into hierarchical event structures and temporal profiles, capturing coherent episodic contexts and tracking dynamically evolving user attributes over time. During retrieval, the system activates an associative memory graph to connect fragmented memories. Experiments on the LoCoMo benchmark confirm that AtomMem achieves state-of-the-art performance across various reasoning tasks, offering a scalable and economically viable solution for deploying intelligent personalized agents.
2606.19847.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）展現出強大的推理和生成能力，但其固定的上下文窗口限制了在多次互動中長期信息的積累和重用。現有的增強記憶系統通常以粗糙和不穩定的方式構建記憶，依賴於低效的記憶表示或不穩定的無約束更新。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了AtomMem，一個為價值密集存儲和穩定記憶演變而設計的長期記憶系統。AtomMem引入了一個事實執行器，選擇性地從長形式互動中提取高價值的原子事實，以作為高效的記憶表示。隨後，AtomMem將這些事實組織成層次事件結構和時間檔案，捕捉連貫的情節上下文並隨時間追蹤動態演變的用戶屬性。在檢索過程中，系統啟動一個聯想記憶圖，以連接碎片化的記憶。在LoCoMo基準上的實驗確認AtomMem在各種推理任務中達到了最先進的性能，提供了一個可擴展且經濟可行的解決方案，用於部署智能個性化代理。
2606.19847.author: Yanyu Yao et.al.
2606.19847.authors: Yanyu Yao, Shangze Li, Zhi Zheng, Hui Zheng, Qi Liu, Tong Xu, Enhong Chen
